sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the center cell (rows 54-56, cols 29-31) is now N (light green)! This is a big change. The cursor position on the bar determined that the center cell would be toggled to green.
So the game mechanic is:
1. The bar has a cursor that can be moved with LEFT/RIGHT
2. The cursor position determines which grid cell is "selected"
3. Clicking toggles the selected cell (white → green, or green → white/gray)
Let me verify this by moving the cursor to different positions and clicking to see which cells get toggled.
The grid is a 3x3 layout (with 3x3 pixel blocks per cell):
- Cell (0,0): rows 49-51, cols 24-26 (gray)
- Cell (0,1): rows 49-51, cols 29-31 (white)
- Cell (0,2): rows 49-51, cols 34-36 (gray)
- Cell (1,0): rows 54-56, cols 24-26 (white)
- Cell (1,1): rows 54-56, cols 29-31 (now green!)
- Cell (1,2): rows 54-56, cols 34-36 (white)
- Cell (2,0): rows 59-61, cols 24-26 (gray)
- Cell (2,1): rows 59-61, cols 29-31 (white)
- Cell (2,2): rows 59-61, cols 34-36 (gray)
The target pattern (left panel) has:
- Cell (0,1): purple (active)
- Cell (1,0): purple (active)
- Cell (1,1): purple (active)
- Cell (1,2): purple (active)
- Cell (2,1): purple (active)
So the target is a cross pattern with 5 active cells.
The current grid has:
- Cell (0,1): white (active)
- Cell (1,0): white (active)
- Cell (1,1): green (active - just toggled)
- Cell (1,2): white (active)
- Cell (2,1): white (active)
The cursor was at the center position when I clicked, and it toggled the center cell to green. So the cursor position maps to the grid cell position!
Let me now figure out the mapping between cursor position on the bar and grid cells. The bar has positions from left to right, and the cursor moves with LEFT/RIGHT. The grid has 9 cells in a 3x3 layout.
Let me try moving the cursor to different positions and clicking to see which cells get toggled. This will help me understand the mapping.
Actually, looking at the bar more carefully:
- The bar has a "track" from cols ~14 to ~42
- The cursor is 2 cells wide (bbSS)
- The cursor can be at different positions along the track
Let me count the possible cursor positions. The track seems to be about 28 cells wide, and the cursor is 2 cells wide. So there are about 26 possible positions. But there are only 9 grid cells, so maybe the cursor moves in steps of ~3 cells per grid cell.
Let me try moving the cursor and clicking to map the positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try to map cursor positions to grid cells
# First, let me reset the grid by clicking on the left panel
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 14}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
